summary: Decompiled 24 functions across many item modules, mainly Pokémon and fighter projectile/item behavior files. The PR replaced placeholder markers with matching C implementations, added or refined item variable and attribute structs in itCommonItems.h, upgraded several item headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed prototypes, and adjusted includes needed for baselib random, effects, stage, debug link, and character-specific item callbacks. The submitter reported successful ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches. Review feedback identified an important struct-design issue: do not overload a generic Pokémon ItemVars union with incompatible field unions; create separate ItemVars unions for each Pokémon type.
what_changed: Replaced placeholder markers for item functions across itgreatfoxlaser, itkabigon, itlugia, itnesspkthundertrail, itoldottosea, ittincle, itnesspkflush, itdosei, itpikachutjoltair, itnessyoyo, itpatapata, and itwhitebea.

Added or refined special-attribute and per-item variable layouts in itCommonItems.h, including itLugiaAttributes extra f32 fields at +0x14/+0x18, itTincleAttributes, itKabigon_ItemVars, itWhiteBea_ItemVars x44, and itWhiteBeaAttributes x10.
Changed itdosei item variable xDE0 from s32 to f32, matching later use as a floating parameter passed through ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei.xDE0.

Implemented itdosei thrown and damage-dealt behavior, including motion-state transition, revealing hidden child JObj, animation frame speed setup, random sound/effect selection through HSD_Randi, and bounce-off-victim handling when msid is 5.
Implemented Great Fox laser physics for a pending direction flip: invert facing_dir, update item orientation, reset state variables x34/x36, and request animation state 1.
Implemented Kabigon setup, animation, timer/effect logic, and effect spawning using item attributes, stage blast-zone checks, HSD_Randf, and efSync_Spawn.
Corrected Kabigon code to use ip->xDD4_itemVar.kabigon fields rather than generic pokemon fields for xE44 timing.
Implemented Lugia motion 4 physics using special attributes, vertical velocity accumulation, a cap against extern f32 it_804DD450, and transition via it_802D1A44 when capped.
Implemented Ness yoyo callbacks for applying material animation to a stored JObj and cleanup/unlink behavior that notifies Ness via ftNs_AttackHi4_YoyoItemSetFlag and deletes linked GObjs.
Implemented collision/transition helpers for Old Ottosea, Patapata, and Pikachu thunder jolt air, including DB linking via db_80225DD8 and item state setup calls.

Updated item headers to replace untyped prototypes with concrete signatures, such as it_802CA074(Item_GObj*), it_802D1A44(Item_GObj*), it_802E0D9C(Item_GObj*), it_802B43D0(Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*), and a fully named it_802AC43C signature.
Adjusted float literal casing in Ness PK Flash and PK Thunder Trail code from 0.0f/1.0f/2.0f to 0.0F/1.0F/2.0F, likely for project style or matching consistency.
smart_moves: Used specific item variable namespaces such as ip->xDD4_itemVar.kabigon, dosei, lugia, patapata, and nesspkthundertrail instead of treating all item storage as generic raw memory.
Promoted placeholder prototypes to typed function signatures in headers as each function became understood, improving future callsite matching and reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS spread.

Localized new includes to the files needing them: baselib/random for HSD_Randi/HSD_Randf, ef/efsync for effect spawning, gr/stage for blast-zone checks, db/db for debug owner linking, and fighter headers for Ness yoyo callbacks.

Matched item behavior by preserving engine helper calls and side effects: Item_80268E5C state changes, it_8026B3A8 reset, it_80272940 item setup, lb_8000BA0C animation speed, HSD_JObj animation functions, and HSD_GObjPLink deletion.
For Kabigon effects, copied ip->pos to a local Vec3 before randomizing effect spawn coordinates, a common matching pattern when assembly uses stack-local position temporaries.
For yoyo cleanup, iterated the ItemLink list by saving link->next before deleting the current linked GObj, avoiding invalid traversal.
decomp_lessons: Per-Pokémon item variable storage should be modeled as distinct ItemVars unions/struct entries when fields differ; forcing multiple Pokémon into a single generic itPokemon_ItemVars with anonymous type punning can be structurally wrong even if it helps one function match.

When an item's specialAttributes are read at fixed offsets, introduce a local attributes struct only to the degree supported by the function evidence, and keep unknown fields named by offsets until semantics are known.

Using exact item state helpers and flags is often the key to matching item behavior functions: msid checks, xDAC/xDB0/xDB4 itcmd vars, xDCC_flag.b3, xDC8_word flags, and x5D0_animFrameSpeed all affect codegen and behavior.
Stage/environment helpers such as Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset can explain transition conditions in Pokémon item animation code.

Randomized visual/audio effects often reveal both required baselib includes and attribute field types; HSD_Randi implies integer table indexing while HSD_Randf with arithmetic around an attribute strongly suggests f32 radius/range fields.
Header cleanup is not just cosmetic in decomp work: replacing UNK signatures can unlock typed callsites and prevent later accidental casts.
Float literal style and exact F suffixes can be relevant for consistency and sometimes matching in this codebase.
PAD_STACK remains an accepted matching tactic where stack shape is not yet naturally reproduced, as seen in Old Ottosea and Ness yoyo snippets.
naming_conventions: Item behavior functions retain address-style names when semantics are unknown, e.g. it_802C9D40, it_802CA3F4, it_802B43D0, and it_802E0D9C.
Known motion callbacks use item and state names such as itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys, itKabigon_UnkMotion0_Anim, itGreatfoxlaser_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itTincle_UnkMotion4_Phys.
Per-item variable structs follow item-specific names such as itDosei_ItemVars, itKabigon_ItemVars, itTincle_ItemVars, and itWhiteBea_ItemVars.
Attribute structs use item-specific names such as itLugiaAttributes, itTincleAttributes, itKabigonAttributes, and itWhiteBeaAttributes.
Unknown fields are kept as offset-style names like x0, x4, x18, xDD4, xDE0, xE44, and xE48 rather than inventing semantics.
Header prototypes include address comments, e.g. /* 2CA074 */ void it_802CA074(Item_GObj* gobj);
Function parameters were often named for role once understood, e.g. Fighter_GObj* gobj, Item_GObj* ball, Vec3* pos, s32 trail_idx, and f32 facing_dir in it_802AC43C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used typed access through GET_ITEM(gobj) or gobj->user_data to align item pointer loads with expected codegen.
Used local scoped temporaries such as f32 speed and Vec3 pos where needed to match assembly stack/register behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in at least itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Coll and it_802BE958 to preserve stack layout.

Preserved exact helper-call ordering in state transitions, such as it_8026B390, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, Item_80268E5C, animation speed assignment, lb_8000BA0C, and it_80274484 in itdosei thrown behavior.
Used explicit casts where call signatures still cross HSD_GObj/Item_GObj boundaries, e.g. Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) gobj, 0, 2).
Modeled random range math as repeated HSD_Randf calls assigned to a local f32 r before modifying Vec3 coordinates.
Kept extern globals where needed for matching, including it_803F56B8[] and it_804DD450.
Represented animation material data with specific baselib types such as HSD_MatAnimJoint* when applying yoyo JObj animation.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: This is wrong; create a new `ItemVars` union for each type of Pokémon.
review_feedback.lesson: Avoid broad anonymous unions inside a generic Pokémon ItemVars struct to cover incompatible layouts; create separate item-specific ItemVars entries for each Pokémon type.

searchable_terms: create a new ItemVars union for each type of Pokémon
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refactor itPokemon_ItemVars into separate Pokémon ItemVars unions after ribbanya review feedback.
Search for itKabigon_ItemVars usages to confirm the final accepted layout of Kabigon timer/xE44/xE48 fields.
Search for itWhiteBeaAttributes x10 and itWhiteBea_ItemVars x44 to see whether later PRs assigned semantic names.
Search for remaining /// # placeholders in the same item files after PR 2281.
Search for other PRs using PAD_STACK in item decomp functions to compare matching tactics.
